perturbation.original_text: Effective Date and Term. This agreement shall begin upon the date of its execution by MA and acceptance in writing by Company and shall remain in effect until the end of the current calendar year and shall be automatically renewed for successive one (1) year periods unless otherwise terminated according to the cancellation or termination provisions contained in paragraph 18 of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Effective Date and Term. This agreement shall begin upon the date of its execution by MA and acceptance in writing by Company and shall remain in effect until the end of the current calendar year. Renewal of this agreement shall be at the sole discretion of the Company and may not be automatically renewed, regardless of the cancellation or termination provisions contained in paragraph 18 of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the agreement automatically renews for one-year periods unless terminated per paragraph 18. The modified version removes the automatic renewal, stating renewal is at the Company's discretion. This contradicts the expectation of automatic renewal, creating uncertainty about the agreement's duration and the MA's rights regarding continued affiliation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Effective Date and Term. This agreement shall begin upon the date of its execution by MA and acceptance in writing by Company and shall remain in effect until the end of the current calendar year. Renewal of this agreement shall be at the sole discretion of the Company and may not be automatically renewed, regardless of the cancellation or termination provisions contained in paragraph 18 of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement would automatically renew for successive one-year periods unless terminated according to the cancellation or termination provisions. The changed text eliminates this automatic renewal and states that renewal is at the sole discretion of the Company, regardless of the termination provisions. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but the contradiction arises from the original agreement stating one thing, and the final agreement stating another, in the same location.

perturbation.original_text: MA shall protect copyrights, tradenames, trademarks, service marks, trade secrets and other confidential proprietary rights and information of Company and its affiliates and report promptly any infringements or suspected infringements of which MA becomes aware and to cooperate fully with Company in its efforts to protect its copyrights, tradenames, trademarks, service marks, trade secrets and other confidential proprietary rights and information.
perturbation.changed_text: MA shall protect copyrights, tradenames, trademarks, service marks, trade secrets and other confidential proprietary rights and information of Company and its affiliates; however, MA's obligation to report infringements is limited to those that directly and significantly impact MA's sales within its territory. The cooperation fully with Company in its efforts to protect its copyrights, tradenames, trademarks, service marks, trade secrets and other confidential proprietary rights and information is based solely on MA's discression.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates MA to protect and report all infringements of Company's intellectual property. The modification limits this obligation, stating that infringements only need to be reported if they significantly impact MA's sales and the cooperation fully with Company in its efforts to protect its copyrights, tradenames, trademarks, service marks, trade secrets and other confidential proprietary rights and information is based solely on MA's discression. This creates an in-text contradiction because it creates uncertainty about whether MA is actually obliged to report any type of infringment that doesn't impact MA's sales.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MA shall protect copyrights, tradenames, trademarks, service marks, trade secrets and other confidential proprietary rights and information of Company and its affiliates; however, MA's obligation to report infringements is limited to those that directly and significantly impact MA's sales within its territory. The cooperation fully with Company in its efforts to protect its copyrights, tradenames, trademarks, service marks, trade secrets and other confidential proprietary rights and information is based solely on MA's discression.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates MA to report all infringements. The changed text limits this obligation to only those significantly impacting MA's sales and limits the cooperation based solely on MA's discression. This directly contradicts the original obligation to report all infringements, creating an in-text contradiction as it introduces uncertainty about whether MA is obligated to report infringements not impacting its sales.
